Name: Accelerator
Definition: IP dedicated to offload a specific workload to enhance performance
Examples: DSP, TPU, packet processing, mathematical, compression

Name: Analog & Mixed-Signal
Definition: IP that controls or senses the electricals for communication, which receives or transmits signals conditioned outside of a system's digital domain

Name: Audio/Video
Definition: IP designed to manipulate audio/video data

Name: Bus/Interface
Definition: IP implementing an interconnect among elements in and/or within a computing system
Examples: I2C, PCIe, DDR, MMC, USB, GPIO, AXI

Name: Controllers
Definition: A circuit hard-wired (e.g. Finite State Machine) to react in a closed-loop control system or other limited context, to control another entity
Examples: Arbiter, APIC, USB, Peripheral, Memory, Storage

Name: Counter/Timer
Definition: IP reflecting the passage of time in oscillations or human units
Examples: Real Time Clock, Watchdog, Monotonic Counter

Name: Microcontroller
Definition: A specialized processor acting as a programmable controller

Name: Power Management
Definition: IP which controls and/or monitors the power state of a system
Examples: Voltage regulators, power controllers or monitors

Name: Security
Examples: Cryptography, authorization, tamper detection, access controls, RNG

Name: Storage
Examples: EEPROM, eFuse, flash, ROM, OTP, NVRAM

Name: Test/Debug
Definition: IP designed to verify functionality and identify root cause of defects
Examples: JTAG, BIST, boundary scan, pattern generator

Name: Transducers
Definition: IP which converts energy from one form to another, such as physical to electrical
